channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump's new Justice Department leadership orders a freeze on civil rights cases
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's new Justice Department leadership has put a freeze on civil rights litigation and suggested it may reconsider police reform agreements negotiated by the Biden administration, according to two memos obtained Wednesday by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Texas man arrested after new homeowners found VHS tapes with child porn
item.description: James Wilkinson, 74, was arrested and accused of possessing at least 500 files that showed child sexual abuse material, according to court records obtained by EverythingLubbock.com.
item.dc:creator: Caitlyn Rooney, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Teen fatally shoots a female student and himself at Antioch High School in Nashville, police say
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — A shooting in a Nashville high school cafeteria Wednesday left a female student dead and another student wounded, nearly two years after another deadly school shooting in the city that ignited an emotional debate about gun control in Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: KRISTIN M. HALL, TRAVIS LOLLER and JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Hughes Fire forces partial evacuation of 5,000+ person Los Angeles jail
item.description: The fast-moving Hughes Fire has forced the partial evacuation of a 5,000+ inmate jail in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Newsom navigates his relationship with Trump as wildfires ravage his state
item.description: Just two days before Air Force One touches down in California, Gov. Gavin Newsom doesn't know if he's welcome to join President Donald Trump's wildfire tour.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Nancy Leftenant-Colon, who was the first Black woman in Army Nurse Corps, has died
item.description: The first Black woman to join the U.S. Army Nurse Corps after the military was desegregated in the 1940s has died. She was 104.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Child dies, officer critical in local shooting
item.description: A 4-year-old child has died and a police officer is in critical condition following an officer-involved shooting in Columbiana County Wednesday
item.dc:creator: Gerry Ricciutti, NewsNation

item.title: Border Patrol agent killed in Vermont worked at the Pentagon during 9/11, family says
item.description: A U.S. Border Patrol agent who was killed in Vermont during a traffic stop near the Canadian border was a military veteran who worked security duty at the Pentagon during the Sept. 11 attacks, his family said.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK and STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Texas man who tried to scam ex-US Rep. George Santos sentenced to 18 months in prison
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A Texas man was sentenced to a year and a half in prison Wednesday over his effort to dupe George Santos out of nearly $1 million by offering to destroy evidence in the disgraced congressman’s corruption case.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Wisconsin man accused of setting fire to congressman's office over TikTok ban charged with arson
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Prosecutors charged a Wisconsin man who allegedly told police he tried to burn down a congressman's office because he was upset with the federal TikTok ban with multiple counts on Wednesday, including arson.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Oath Keepers founder Stewart Rhodes visits Capitol Hill after Trump clemency
item.description: Oath Keepers founder Stewart Rhodes, the far-right extremist group leader convicted of seditious conspiracy in the Jan. 6, 2021, attack, has visited Capitol Hill after President Donald Trump commuted his 18-year prison sentence.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kunzelman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Teen fatally shoots 16-year-old girl, himself at Tennessee high school: police
item.description: A student is dead and another injured after a teen gunman opened fire at a Nashville area high school before turning the gun on himself, police say.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, KTLA 5

item.title: Starbucks' policy change flushes out a debate over public restroom access
item.description: Starbucks’ decision to restrict its restrooms to paying customers has flushed out a wider problem: a patchwork of restroom policies that has left Americans confused and divided over who gets to use the loo and when. Rules about restroom access in restaurants vary by state, city and county.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Teen fatally shoots 16-year-old girl, himself at Tennessee high school: police
item.description: A student is dead and another injured after a teen gunman opened fire at a Nashville area high school before turning the gun on himself, police say.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man who got Jan. 6 pardon is arrested on federal gun charge
item.description: The Florida man appears to be the first Jan. 6 rioter to face additional legal trouble after the sweeping clemency bestowed on those who rioted at the Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KRON4

item.title: Trump pardoned violent Jan. 6 rioters. Here are 5 of them
item.description: Hundreds of rioters accused of violently assaulting police at the Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021, were pardoned Monday by President Trump, despite remarks from his allies in recent weeks condemning those defendants.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: GOP leaders stare down legislative minefield after Trump's Day 1 blitz
item.description: President Trump made a big splash with dozens of executive actions on his first day in office, but now comes the hard part: getting Republicans in Congress in line to usher an ambitious legislative agenda through their slim majorities and clashing factions. Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration shuts down White House Spanish-language page and social media
item.description: Within hours of President Donald Trump’s inauguration, the new administration took down the Spanish-language version of the official White House website. The site — currently https://www.whitehouse.gov/es/ — now gives users an “Error 404” message.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Previously approved refugee flights to US canceled after Trump order
item.description: The U.S. refugee program is considered one of the most rigorous on the globe, providing a pathway to safe haven for those fleeing danger or who has faced persecution due to their race, religion, nationality or political views.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Justice Department directs prosecutors to probe local efforts to obstruct immigration enforcement
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department is directing its federal prosecutors to investigate for potential criminal charges any state or local officials who stand in the way of beefed-up enforcement of immigration laws under the Trump administration, according to a memo to the workforce obtained by
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Cat mistakenly left in plane cargo hold makes 3 trips in 24 hours
item.description: A Maine coon cat named Mittens became an accidental jetsetter this month when her cage was overlooked in a plane cargo hold and she made three trips in 24 hours between New Zealand and Australia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: GOP congressman wants bishop 'added to deportation list' after Trump prayer service
item.description: Rep. Mike Collins heavily criticized statements made by the Right Rev. Mariann Budde on Tuesday at the inaugural prayer service held for President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: Trump orders FAA to terminate DEI hiring efforts
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday issued an executive order directing the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to "return to non-discriminatory, merit-based hiring" and eliminate diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) initiatives.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump deportations come after illegal immigrant rattled South Carolina; motive in interstate shutdown unknown
item.description: South Carolina Republicans slammed the Biden administration's border policies after an alleged bomb hoax by an illegal immigrant from Jordan shut a highway down for hours.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans renew efforts to limit people in US illegally from census count
item.description: Republican efforts to exclude people in the U.S. illegally from numbers used to divvy up congressional seats among states have begun anew, with four Republican state attorneys general suing to alter the once-a-decade head count even before President Donald Trump’s second term in office began Monday.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A frigid storm drops rare snow on Houston and New Orleans as Florida readies plows in the Panhandle
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — A rare frigid storm charged through Texas and the northern Gulf Coast on Tuesday, blanketing New Orleans and Houston with snow that closed highways, grounded nearly all flights and canceled school for more than a million students more accustomed to hurricane dismissals than snow d
item.dc:creator: GERALD HERBERT and DAVID J. PHILLIP, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: As fire-fanning winds continue, Southern California readies for potential rain and toxic runoff
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Parched Southern California continued to face dangerous winds but could get some badly needed rain this weekend, forecasters said Tuesday, dampening the prospects of another round of killer wildfires though even a small amount of precipitation could could create new challenges lik
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump rolls back Biden directive to study methods of lowering prescription costs
item.description: Among the dozens of Biden-era executive orders he rescinded on day one, President Trump nixed one that aimed to explore new health care payment models that would lower prescription costs for Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, Fox 5

item.title: Inauguration prayer service's pastor asks Trump to 'have mercy' on transgender kids, immigrants
item.description: “I ask you to have mercy upon the people in our country who are scared. There are gay, lesbian, transgender children, Democratic, Republican, independent families — some who fear for their lives,” she said.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KRON4

item.title: Trump wants to pull the US out of the World Health Organization again. Here's what may happen next
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump used one of the flurry of executive actions that he issued on his first day back in the White House to begin the process of withdrawing the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARIA CHENG and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Garth Hudson, The Band's last surviving member, dies at 87
item.description: Garth Hudson, the Band's virtuoso keyboardist and all-around musician who drew from a unique palette of sounds and styles to add a conversational touch to such rock standards as "Up on Cripple Creek," “The Weight” and "Rag Mama Rag," has died at age 87.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Why Biden pardoned Mark Milley ahead of Trump taking office
item.description: Former President Joe Biden's pre-emptive pardon for retired Gen. Mark Milley, the former chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, will give the retired military official a shield against any action that President Donald Trump might take against him amid their highly public feud.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, Fox 5
